sample_size.source_quote: The study population consisted of 138 adult patients (aged > 18 years) who fulfilled the Sepsis-3 criteria

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: The median SOFA score at the baseline was 5 (IQR of 4–7)

mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 1.316 per one-unit increase (31.6% increase in odds of mortality)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, WBC, NLR, LAC_Base, SOFA_Base, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: for each one-unit increase in the CCI, the odds of mortality increased by 31.6%

mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 1.47 per one-unit increase (47% increase in odds of mortality)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, WBC, CCI, LAC_Base, SOFA_Base, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: a one-unit increase in the NLR was associated with a 47% increase in the odds of mortality

mortality_predictors.effect_size: Each one-unit increase more than doubling the odds of mortality
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, WBC, CCI, NLR, SOFA_Base, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The baseline lactate level emerged as a particularly strong predictor, with each one-unit increase more than doubling the odds of mortality.

mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 1.735 per one-unit increase (73.5% increase in odds of mortality)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, WBC, CCI, NLR, LAC_Base, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the baseline SOFA score demonstrated a substantial impact, with each one-unit increase leading to a 73.5% increase in the odds of mortality

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: PCT percentage change from baseline to 72h (PCT_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 0.948 per 1% decrease (5.2% reduction in mortality odds per 1% decrease)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the odds ratios of 0.948, 0.762, and 0.887, respectively ... a 1% decrease in the PCT level corresponds to a 5.2% reduction in mortality odds (1 −0.948 = 0.052)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: CRP percentage change from baseline to 72h (CRP_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 0.762 per 1% decrease (24% reduction in mortality odds per 1% decrease)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the odds ratio of 0.762 for the CRP_Delta signifies that for every 1% decrease in CRP levels over the first three days, the odds of mortality are reduced by approximately 24% (1 −0.762 = 0.238)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Lactate percentage change from baseline to 72h (LAC_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR: 0.887 per 1% decrease (11.3% reduction in mortality odds per 1% decrease)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: a 1% decrease in the LAC level translates to an 11.3% reduction in mortality odds (1 −0.887 = 0.113)
Among the baseline predictors, the SOFA score exhibited the highest discriminatory power (AUC = 0.762), followed by age (0.730) and NLR (0.716)
the CCI (0.609) and WBC (0.546) demonstrated weaker predictive capabilities, with the WBC approaching the level of a random classifier (AUC = 0.5)

The AUCs for the percentage decreases in the PCT, CRP, and LAC levels, from baseline to M3 (PCT_Delta: 0.843, CRP_Delta: 0.903, and LAC_Delta: 0.703), were notably higher than those for their corresponding baseline values (PCT_Base: 0.478, CRP_Base: 0.540, and LAC_Base: 0.689)
